title: Artanthe cassinoides
intro: Artanthe cassinoides, also known as the Cassina plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Celastraceae. This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including gastrointestinal problems, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Artanthe cassinoides include flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Artanthe cassinoides is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, or used in the form of a tincture or syrup to treat its various health benefits.
taxonomy: Artanthe cassinoides is classified within the plant family Passifloraceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the passionflower family.
